column1: Subclass 190 Tasmanian Skilled Employment (TSE) Priority Roles

Applicants must have a valid skills assessment for an occupation listed in the “TSE Priority Occupations - eligible skills assessments” column to be eligible for the Tasmanian Skilled Employment Pathway under subclass 190.
Applicants must also be working in a closely related role - one that falls in to the same ANZSCO 3-digit group. Examples of roles in the same group are listed in the second column.

For example, if someone has a skills assessment for Management Consultant but are employed as an Economist they may be eligible for subclass 190 nomination in the Tasmanian Skilled Employment Pathway.

TSE Priority Occupations - eligible skills assessments
Must have a skills assessment for one of these occupations
column2:
Examples of related roles
Not all occupations in the 3-digit ANZSCO group have been listed
